name: Larrasa and Cruz del Castillo through the port of Toro — circular via the charcoal burners' path (PR-A-50)
name_original: Larrasa y Cruz del Castillo por el puerto del Toro — circular por la senda de las carboneras (PR-A-50)
region_intro: Hiking around Lagrán, located in the Montaña Alavesa region of Álava, Spain, offers diverse landscapes for outdoor activities. The area is characterized by the Sierra de Toloño mountain range, which provides a scenic backdrop and varied terrain. Hikers can find routes ranging from accessible paths on lower slopes to demanding ascents, including trails leading to Larrasa Peak, the second-highest point in Álava. The region features a mix of landscapes, from moderate trails to routes requiring sure-footedness and sturdy footwear. Best hiking trails around Lagrán The most popular hiking route is Larrasa and Cruz del Castillo through the port of Toro — circular via the charcoal burners' path (PR-A-50), a 6.0 miles (9.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 11 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes significant elevation gain and leads to panoramic views from Larrasa Peak. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cocinas, Artxabal and Zelarruntxa — circular through the southern mountain ranges of Álava, a moderate 7.5 miles (12.0 km) path. This circular route explores the southern mountain ranges of Álava, offering varied scenery. Local hikers also love the Pipaón Trail (PR-A 53), a 6.8 miles (11.0 km) trail leading through the southern mountains of Álava, often completed in about 3 hours 21 minutes. Hiking around Lagrán is defined by the Sierra de Toloño mountain range, Larrasa Peak, and varied terrain with demanding ascents. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate walks to challenging summit trails. The routes in Lagrán are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 650 hikers have used komoot to explore Lagrán's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Lagrán?
A: There are over 20 hiking trails around Lagrán, offering a variety of experiences. These include 5 easy routes, 11 moderate routes, and 7 difficult routes, catering to different fitness levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking in Lagrán?
A: The terrain around Lagrán is diverse, ranging from accessible paths on lower slopes to demanding ascents within the Sierra de Toloño mountain range. Some routes, especially those leading to Larrasa Peak, require sure-footedness and sturdy footwear due to significant elevation changes and rocky sections.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Lagrán?
A: Yes, Lagrán offers several excellent circular routes. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051957397/cocinas-artxabal-and-zelarruntxa-circular-through-the-southern-mountain-ranges-of-alava" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cocinas, Artxabal and Zelarruntxa — circular through the southern mountain ranges of Álava&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 12.0 km. For a more challenging experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050778474/larrasa-and-cruz-del-castillo-through-the-port-of-toro-circular-via-the-charcoal-burners-path-pr-a-50" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Larrasa and Cruz del Castillo through the port of Toro — circular via the charcoal burners' path (PR-A-50)&lt;/a&gt;, a 9.7 km difficult hike.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or natural monuments to see on the trails?
A: Yes, the area around Lagrán features several natural attractions. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2905300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Water Route&lt;/a&gt;, or visit specific waterfalls like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5474808" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ollerías Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6864843" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Inglares Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5220027" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ayuda River Gorge&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable natural feature. For impressive views, the summit of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3223322" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toloño&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent natural monument.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Lagrán?
A: Lagrán offers hiking opportunities throughout the year, but the best time often depends on your preferred experience. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Autumn, in particular, is praised for its beautiful colors. Some routes, especially those at higher elevations, may require specific gear or experience during winter due to snow or ice.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Lagrán?
A: While some routes are challenging, Lagrán does offer more moderate trails suitable for families. These trails often explore the lower slopes and southern mountain ranges of Álava, providing enjoyable walks without extreme difficulty. Always check the route's specific details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's fitness level.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Lagrán?
A: Many trails in natural areas are generally dog-friendly, but it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or protected wildlife. Ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you carry waste bags. Specific rules may apply in certain natural parks or reserves, so check local signage.

Q: Where can I find parking for the hiking trails?
A: Parking is typically available near trailheads in and around Lagrán. For example, if you plan to hike the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050789991/pipaon-trail-pr-a-53" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pipaón Trail (PR-A 53)&lt;/a&gt;, you would look for parking in or near Pipaón. For routes starting directly from Lagrán, village parking options are usually available. It's recommended to check specific starting points for designated parking areas.

Q: Is public transport available to access hiking trails in Lagrán?
A: Public transport options to Lagrán and its surrounding trailheads may be limited. While local bus services might connect Lagrán to larger towns in Álava, direct access to specific trail starting points often requires a car. It's best to research current bus schedules and routes if you plan to rely on public transport.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Lagrán?
A: The hiking experiences in Lagrán are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 70 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the stunning views from peaks like Larrasa, and the variety of trails that cater to different skill levels, from moderate walks to challenging ascents.

Q: Are there any challenging day hikes in the Lagrán area?
A: Yes, Lagrán offers several challenging day hikes. The ascent to Larrasa Peak, the second-highest point in Álava, is a demanding but rewarding experience. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050778474/larrasa-and-cruz-del-castillo-through-the-port-of-toro-circular-via-the-charcoal-burners-path-pr-a-50" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Larrasa and Cruz del Castillo through the port of Toro&lt;/a&gt;, with its significant elevation gain, are considered difficult and require good fitness and sure-footedness.

Q: Can I find historical sites or landmarks along the trails?
A: Yes, beyond natural beauty, the region also has points of historical interest. For example, you can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4131818" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen of the Witch's Hut&lt;/a&gt;, an ancient megalithic monument, which can be incorporated into some hiking routes, offering a glimpse into the area's past.

Q: Are there any easy hikes suitable for a relaxed day out?
A: Yes, among the 23 total routes, there are 5 classified as easy. These trails typically feature less elevation gain and shorter distances, making them perfect for a relaxed day out or for those new to hiking. They often explore the more accessible parts of the landscape around Lagrán.
